summary: Iteration 3: Verified swipe fix + 4 new sub-pages. Backend 5/5 pytest pass (AI chat, status CRUD, health). Sub-pages (Customers, Inventory, Reports, Payouts) all render correctly with required content (8 customers, 12 inventory items, $1284 hero, $612.40 next payout, Iced Latte/Croissant top sellers, 6 payouts, all stat cards, search filters working, customer detail expansion, Low filter works). Back button navigates to /more. 'Coming soon' sonner toast fires for non-routed tiles. AI assistant works end-to-end (Claude Sonnet 4.5 returns $1,284.50). Swipe-from-keypad correctly switches segments WITHOUT firing digit press (root fix verified). HOWEVER: a CRITICAL REGRESSION was introduced — a plain TAP on any keypad key (key-0..9, C, del) no longer registers because setPointerCapture on pointerdown redirects the synthesized click event to the swipe-carousel div, bypassing the inner button's onClick. Confirmed via instrumented event listeners: pointerdown fires on key-5, but the click event fires on swipe-carousel DIV. Result: users can NEVER enter an amount via the keypad — this blocks the entire Key In / Charge flow.

ui_bugs.issue: CRITICAL REGRESSION: setPointerCapture called in onPointerDown causes the synthesized click event from a normal tap to fire on the swipe-carousel DIV instead of the inner keypad button. Result: tapping key-0..9, C, or del does NOTHING (amount stays $0.00). The Key In flow is completely broken — users cannot enter charge amounts. Original swipe-while-tapping bug is fixed, but the fix overshoots and kills regular taps.

action_items: After fix, manually verify: (a) tap key-5 -> $0.05; (b) press-and-hold key-5 then drag left -> switches to Catalog WITHOUT entering 5; (c) swipe right from Catalog -> Key In.
critical_code_review_comments: HomePage.jsx: setPointerCapture in onPointerDown is too aggressive — synthesized click after pointerup is routed to the captured element (carousel), bypassing button onClick. Defer capture until horizontal motion is confirmed.
ReportsPage.jsx (281 lines), HomePage.jsx (545 lines): files are getting large; consider extracting Segmented + KeyInView + CatalogView into /components/home/.

MorePage.jsx Tile component: route map is hardcoded in MorePage instead of in mock data; would be cleaner to add `route?: string` per item in moreSections and let absence of route trigger the toast — keeps tile-list and routing single-sourced.
Reports/Inventory/Payouts pages: bar chart, donut, sparkbars are all inline SVG/divs — fine for mock, but extract a tiny <BarChart/> primitive when wiring real data.
Customer search filter is case-sensitive in some places — consider .toLowerCase() on both sides if not already.

success_rate.frontend: 92% — 4 new sub-pages + swipe gesture all OK, but keypad tap regression blocks Key In flow

context_for_next_testing_agent: Re-test (1) `await page.click('[data-testid="key-5"]')` increments amount-display to $0.05 — currently FAILS; (2) press-and-drag from key-5 still switches to Catalog without entering 5 — currently PASSES; (3) all 4 sub-page testids: customers-page/inventory-page/reports-page/payouts-page render — PASSES. Customer ids in mock are u1..u8 (not c1..c8 as spec implies) — `customer-u1`..`customer-u8`. Inventory item ids and payout ids per mock.js. AI orb has float animation so click must use force=True. Bottom-nav-more selector works for navigating to /more.

rca of the issue.title: Keypad taps no longer register after swipe fix (pointer capture hijacks click)
rca of the issue.reproduction: 1. Open /. 2. Tap data-testid='key-5'. 3. Observe amount-display stays $0.00.
rca of the issue.root_cause: HomePage.jsx onPointerDown unconditionally calls e.currentTarget.setPointerCapture(e.pointerId) on the swipe-carousel DIV. Per the Pointer Events spec, when a pointer is captured by an element, the implicit `click` event generated on pointerup is dispatched to the capturing element (the carousel DIV) instead of the original target (the keypad button). Verified via DOM event instrumentation: pointerdown target = key-5, click target = swipe-carousel DIV. Therefore React's onClick on <button data-testid='key-5'> never fires and `press('5')` is never called.
rca of the issue.mitigation: Defer setPointerCapture until horizontal-drag lock is established in onPointerMove (after |dx|>8 and |dx|>|dy|). For pure taps there is no movement, so capture never engages and the click reaches the button as normal. For swipes (which DO move horizontally before the user lifts), capture engages mid-gesture and remains held for pointerup, preserving the original fix where inner buttons cannot steal pointermove.
